title: Here’s the full lineup of athletes and celebrities involved in Drake Maye’s charity softball game
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Drake Maye's charity softball game at Polar Park in Worcester is scheduled for Sunday afternoon.
Participants include Patriots coaches Mike Vrabel and Josh McDaniels, and celebrities like Julian Edelman and Zdeno Chara.
The event raises funds for the MayeDay foundation, supporting children and families facing hardship.

title: Potential A.J. Brown Patriots-Eagles trade reportedly has ‘framework in place’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Patriots and Eagles reportedly have a trade framework for wide receiver A.J. Brown, pending further details.
A post-June 1 trade of Brown would reduce Philadelphia's salary cap impact from $43.5 million to $16 million.
The Patriots are hesitant to trade a first-round pick, a key demand from the Eagles, potentially delaying the deal.

title: Did you hear it? Here’s what we know about a big boom heard around Mass.
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: A loud boom heard around Greater Boston was likely caused by a bolide meteor entering the atmosphere.
Local meteorologists ruled out an earthquake and confirmed the phenomenon with satellite data from GOES-19 GLM.
Massachusetts Public Safety received numerous 911 calls, but no emergencies were reported in connection to the boom.

title: Brayan Bello excels in bulk relief, but opener melts in Red Sox’ 4-3 loss to Guardians
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox opener Tyler Samaniego allowed four runs in the first inning against the Guardians, leading to a 4-3 loss.
Brayan Bello excelled in relief, pitching seven scoreless innings with five strikeouts and no walks.
The loss dropped the Red Sox to 23-33, marking them 10 games under .500 and last in the AL East.

title: Giannis Antetokounmpo praises TD Garden environment amid trade rumors
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Giannis Antetokounmpo praised Boston Celtics fans and the TD Garden environment in a recent livestream.
Boston Celtics and Miami Heat are considered contenders to land Antetokounmpo amid ongoing trade rumors.
Jaylen Brown could be a key piece in a potential trade for Antetokounmpo, possibly involving a three-team deal.

title: Harvard ‘Senior Sunrise’ cut short by cops
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Police dispersed the Harvard Class of 2026's Senior Sunrise event on Weeks Footbridge due to crowd concerns.
Officers responded to a large group blocking the bridge and attempting to swim in the Charles River.
Cambridge, Harvard University, and Mass. State Police were involved in clearing the early morning gathering.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Kill Bill: The Whole Bloody Affair' is available on Peacock, featuring Uma Thurman's performance across its combined narrative.
'The Truman Show' featuring Jim Carrey streams on Paramount+ and YouTube, examining reality TV's impact with a satirical lens.
'The Four Seasons' Season 2, starring Tina Fey and Will Forte, is now streaming on Netflix, continuing its comedy-drama narrative.

title: Red Sox predicted to draft 18-year-old third baseman in 2026 MLB Draft by ESPN
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox predicted by ESPN's Kiley McDaniel to select Bo Lowrance, an 18-year-old third baseman, in the 2026 MLB Draft.
Bo Lowrance, standing 6-foot-5 and 200 pounds, is a left-handed hitter from South Carolina committed to the University of Virginia.
Lowrance, compared to Freddie Freeman by scouts, is projected to go 20th overall, with questions about his future position.

title: Lowell father and daughter charged in alleged payroll tax scheme
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Lim Ou, 69, and Nalen Ou, 39, of Lowell charged with tax crimes for alleged cash payroll concealment.
Prosecutors allege they used staffing firms to avoid paying over $12 million in federal employment taxes.
Between 2019 and 2025, over $48 million allegedly paid in cash, bypassing tax obligations, officials claim.

title: Report: Christian Gonzalez unlikely to participate in OTAs under current Patriots contract
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Christian Gonzalez seeking contract extension, missing New England Patriots OTAs amid negotiations.
Gonzalez aims for top NFL cornerback salary, currently under rookie contract earning $4 million for 2026.
Patriots clear cap space by restructuring Mike Onwenu's contract, potentially aiding Gonzalez negotiations.
